blocks.title: Activities
content_blocks: This section covers everything important found outside of dungeons that doesn't get elaborated on anywhere else in the guide. Because of the amount of content in this game, some smaller activities won't be covered here as they are usually self explanatory. Hopefully if you can't find something anywhere else, you can find it here.

blocks.title: Bug Catching
content_blocks: Bug catching is a side activity used to help gather fishing bait. There are a few different ways to acquire bugs, and this section will detail all of them. The way to get bugs will change depending on what month it currently is. The three ways to get bugs are through the Bug-Catching Kid, using a net, and gardening during the winter.
Bug-Catching Kid
During April and May, you can talk to the Bug-Catching Kid at Shopping District Shrine to trade an item for Tatshumie Ladybugs. Here is the list of items he wants:
Placeholder (Need to research)
Bug Net

Starting June 5th, you can obtain a net to catch bugs. Talk to Bug-Catching Kid at Shopping District Shrine, then talk to Lady in White at Shopping District Shrine at night, then talk again to Bug-Catching Kid. Now you can catch bugs at the Shopping District Shrine. This involves a small minigame. You have to hit the X button the exact moment the ! appears above your head. The trick to do this is to watch the net, and hit X right when it hits the top of the fence in the background. The better your timing, the more bugs you get. Bugs you get are assumed to be at random, but all of them can be used for fishing. Note that Inaba Jewel Beetle only appears after asking the Old Man at Samegawa Riverbank about the Guardian, and Genji Beetle appears after asking the Old Man about the Sea Guardian.
Gardening
During January and February, gardening is a way to catch bugs. It can be done once a night, and is assumed to be random on what bugs you get.

blocks.title: Fishing
content_blocks: Fishing is a side activity that is needed for a decent amount of quests. There are two places to fish, Samegawa Riverbank and Shichiri Beach, which is unlocked from riding the scooter a few times. The fish you can obtain depends on where you are and what bug you use as bait. The higher your Diligence, the more lines you can cast in one sitting. Here is how to get the fishing rods.
River Rod

The River Rod is required to fish at Samegawa Riverbank. Talk to Bug-Catching Kid at Shopping District Shrine to get a Tatshumie Ladybug. Then obtain some kind of gem, like a Blue Quartz. They randomly drop from enemies. Once you have both, visit the Shiroku Pub at night and give her a Tatshumie Ladybug to get a Fishing Hook and some Bread Crumbs. Talk to Old Man at Samegawa Riverbank to get the River Rod.
Deep-Sea Rod

The Deep-Sea Rod is required to fish at Shichiri Beach. After obtaining the River Rod, ask Old Man at Samegawa Riverbank about Guardian so Inaba Jewel Beetles to become available to catch. Catch a few at Shopping District Shrine, then use them as bait to catch a Guardian. Show the Guardian to Old Man to get the Deep-Sea Rod. Make sure to ask the Old Man about the Sea Guardian so Genji Beetles become available to catch.
Fishing Minigame

First, select the bait you wish to use. Typically, each bait can catch two fish represented by small ripples or big ripples to tell which one you will catch. If the ripples aren't the ones you want, reel in early and cast again. Once the fish bites, hit Circle to hook the fish. Once the fish is hooked, a bar will appear. Use the Left Stick to manipulate the arrow left and right. The bigger the fish, the more resistance it'll have. To reel in the fish, move the arrow to the blue area of the bar, and hold Circle. The longer you reel in, the smaller the blue area becomes. Let go of Circle, and the blue area will grow bigger, but at the cost of the fish pulling farther away. If you hit Circle on the red area multiple times, the line will break and the fish will escape. After reading Expert Fishing, sometimes the Triangle button will show up. Hitting it reels in the fish a big amount. This book is almost required for the guardians.
Fish List

This is the list for everything you can catch, and how to catch them. Note that Shichiri Beach has some joke weapons. You can only catch one joke weapon per day, and they are on a set rotation. Note that Chie is the only one without a joke weapon from fishing.

blocks.title: Gardening
content_blocks: Gardening is a side activity that allows you to harvest items for dungeons. You can access the garden at home, typically during the night. In addition to planting seeds, you can maintain the garden. This will pass time, but will reduce the time needed to harvest the plants, or on the day before harvest increase the amount harvested. This can also increase Diligence and earn points toward Hierophant and Justice. If you wait too long to harvest, the plants will wither and you will lose the items.
Gardening Woman

The Gardening Woman appears either at Junes or the Dojima Residence. Before gardening is unlocked, you can talk to her to buy harvest items. Once gardening is unlocked, she will instead sell seedlings you can plant. As time passes, more plants unlock. Once winter arrives, she will go back to selling harvest items. blocks.title: Cooking
content_blocks: Sometimes when checking the fridge, you will be able to cook lunch for the next day. You must choose the correct response to successfully cook lunch. You can invite a classmate Social Link to share lunch with to earn points. If it's their favorite, they will earn extra points. Here is the list of what you can cook and all related details.

content_blocks: Once the Party Members have access to scooters, they will sometimes be available to go on a trip to the Beach or Hot Springs. These trips allow Party Members to either learn new Skills they otherwise don't have access to, or relearn Skills you decided to get rid of. blocks.title: Chagall Cafe
content_blocks: The Chagall Cafe is located in Okina City and can be used to obtain Skill Cards. After the first visit to the cafe, some Persona Skills will have a card icon. Visiting Chagall Cafe with a Persona equipped that has a Skill with a card icon will let you obtain that Skill as a Skill Card. There are a few special rules for this mechanic. The Persona equipped must have a Skill with a card icon, otherwise nothing happens. After a Skill Card is obtained, the card icon disappears from the skill. Finally, when fusing any Skills inherited with a card icon will lose the icon after fusion. Meaning, card icons are exclusive to that Persona, and can't be passed down through fusion. The list of Personas with card icons are listed under the Skill Cards section.

blocks.title: Crane Machine
content_blocks: In Okina City, there is a crane machine that you can play. There are five grand prizes that can be won, which you can put on the shelf in your room. You can play five times a day, with the prize being won at random. Days ending with 0, 2, or 6 give two plays for the price of one. On the 30th of each month, the chance to win a prize are increased. There are a bunch of items that can be won, but this section will only list the grand prizes. New grand prizes are available on certain days, but won't replace old prizes. Old prizes will need to be won first before the new prizes will be available. Here is the list of grand prizes.
Grand Prize Available Jack Frost Doll June 11th Pyro Jack Doll July 27th Neko Shogun Doll September 12th King Frost Doll November 6th Black Frost Doll January 1st

blocks.title: Models
content_blocks: Models can usually be bought from Marutake Hobby Shop in Shopping District North after completing Quest 11. Models bought from the Hobby Shop can be constructed at the Work Table in your Room. Placing all models of a specific type on the shelf will earn you a stat increase for every Status Parameter. This is the list of models you can make.

blocks.title: Miracle Quiz
content_blocks: The Midnight Trivia Miracle Quiz is optional content located under TV Listings. There are three stages, and each stage must be completed to unlock the next. Winning the third and final stage will earn you a trophy in your room. Each stage consists of twenty multiple choice questions. A correct answer gives one point while an incorrect answer will lose one point. Whoever has the highest score at the end is the winner. If there is a tie, then there is no winner. Here are all of the known questions in the quiz. Note that due to the nature of this quiz, there may be a few questions missing from this list. If you find one not on the list, send me a screenshot and I'll add it to the list.

blocks.title: Other Activities
content_blocks: This section details other small activities that are available throughout the game. Some info on these activities are scarce, since most of them are rarely used.
Scooter
Starting on June 9th, you can ride the scooter next to your house. Riding the scooter will unlock Okina City and Shichiri Beach, as well as increase Courage.
Vending Machine
At the back of Shopping District North there is a vending machine. When buying items there is a random chance to win a roulette to win a free item.
Capsule Machine
Outside of the Shiroku Store in Shopping District South is a capsule machine. This can only be used during rain or snow, and will pass time. Using the capsule machine will give you some items.
Prize Stickers
Prize Stickers are obtained from ordering from the TV Shopping program. You can mail in 3 Prize Stickers at the mailbox in the Shopping District to earn a prize. Here is the list of prizes:
Quantity:
5 Muscle Drink 5 Odd Morsel 5 Rancid Gravy 5 Firecracker 5 Ice Cube 5 Ball Lightning 5 Pinwheel
Quality:
Bead Chain Snuff Soul Chewing Soul Amrita Soda Homunculus
Relationship Fortunes

At the Shopping District Shrine you can draw a Relationship Fortune, which gives points toward a Social Link of your choice. Small gives one point, mild gives two points, and great gives three points. Note that Courage Rank Reliable is needed to enter the Shrine at night.
Chinese Diner Aiya

The Chinese Diner Aiya is located at Shopping District North and offers meals to increase stats (need to research). During rain, there is a special meal that increases three random Status Parameters, except Expression.
Shichiri Beach

In addition to fishing, you can participate in other activities at Shichiri Beach. Gazing out to the sea will give a Persona in your deck their next Skill they will learn. Swimming will increase Courage, and will also increase Understanding during rain.
The shelf in your room can hold figures and models. Putting every model in a set on the shelf will give you a increase in every Status Parameter.
Sleeping in the Futon in your room will give points toward a random Social Link.
After completing Quest 14, a cat will appear next to your house. Spending time with this cat will raise Expression and Understanding.
Vox Populi
Vox Populi is a online feature that shows info from other online players. Vox Populi can be used to see what actions people took on the current day, and what Personas they fused on the current day.
Rescue Request

The Rescue Request and SOS is a online feature that is used during dungeons. You can both send and receive help from online players. Receiving help results in restoring HP and SP to the party. In the Velvet Room, the Rescue Request option lets you customize the message you send to online players.
